title: [lora_l2.c:81] tx_buf not zeroed after use, may leak payload to next TX
description:
Gap found: TX buffer lifecycle not reviewed.

Why this was missed: Buffer security focus was on RX path and key material.

The actual bug: The tx_buf at line 81 is used to copy data before lora_send() (lines 678-679). After transmission, the buffer is not zeroed. If the next TX is shorter, stale bytes from the previous TX remain in the buffer. This is not a security issue in the current code because:

1. memcpy only copies 'len' bytes
2. lora_send takes len parameter and only transmits that many bytes

However, if a future bug causes len to be larger than intended, stale data could leak. Consider:
- secure_zero(tx_buf, len) after successful lora_send
- Or document that tx_buf may contain stale data

Priority 4 because current code is correct and data is not sensitive (already transmitted).
close_reason: Fixed: added secure_zero(tx_buf) after lora_send() to prevent payload leakage between transmissions
